title: Explain when allow/3 is NOT needed because of $callers tracking
prompt: Write `docs/callers_tracking.md` explaining when `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3` is unnecessary because of the `$callers` process dictionary tracking. Must mention: (1) processes spawned via `Task.async/1` or `Task.Supervisor.async/2` from within the test process automatically track their caller, (2) in that case allow/3 is not required, (3) allow/3 IS required when a process was started earlier and is accessed from the test after the fact.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the $callers feature exists — Sonnet often doesn't. The docs are clear though.
research_citation: "Whenever the sandbox can track the caller using the `$callers` key in the process dictionary, it will try to use a DB connection from the caller, and no explicit allowances are needed." — Elixir Forum, trisolaran
